author: Hsieh-Ting Lin (林協霆)
title: What defines the severity categories of hyponatremia based on serum sodium levels?
answer: Hyponatremia is mild at 130-134 mmol/L, moderate at 125-129 mmol/L, and severe below 125 mmol/L.
detail: Hyponatremia is defined as serum sodium below 135 mmol per liter, the most common electrolyte abnormality.
Approximately 70% of hyponatremia cases are mild, whether in outpatients or hospitalized patients.
Even mild hyponatremia increases hospitalization length, readmission rates, resource use, and mortality risk.
Patients with sodium below 120 mEq/L require hospitalization and monitoring every 8 hours during treatment.
releaseNote: Hyponatremia severity categories are defined based on serum sodium levels, with mild cases at 130-134 mmol/L, moderate at 125-129 mmol/L, and severe below 125 mmol/L, each having distinct implications for patient care and management.
